summary:
This file acts as a filter for the team's shared code repository. It defines strict rules about which files and folders should remain private to individual developer machines and should never be uploaded to the central project storage.

**Business Behavior**
- Keeps the shared project workspace lean and organized by automatically excluding temporary, generated, and machine-specific files.
- Prevents sensitive configuration data (like passwords, API keys, and internal settings) from being accidentally committed to the shared repository.
- Reduces storage costs and speeds up code sharing by excluding large dependency libraries and build artifacts that can be safely regenerated by anyone.

**User Impact**
- End users experience more stable and faster software updates because the deployment process only handles essential, production-ready files.
- Security is improved by ensuring customer credentials and internal environment settings never leak into public or shared code stores.
- Developers can work faster and with fewer conflicts, leading to quicker feature releases and bug fixes.

**Rules**
- **Never share:** Log files, temporary process files, test coverage reports, local caches, and framework-specific build folders.
- **Never share:** Third-party libraries and package managers (these are automatically downloaded when needed).
- **Never share:** Local environment files containing secrets (e.g., `.env`), but always keep the template file (`.env.example`) so new developers know what settings are required.
- **Always share:** All source code, configuration templates, and documented setup instructions.

**Data Movement**
- Files matching these rules stay strictly on individual developer computers.
- Only clean, necessary source files and approved templates are pushed to the central repository.
- When a new developer joins or a deployment runs, missing dependencies and build folders are automatically recreated from the shared repository, ensuring consistency without transferring redundant data.

**Visible Outcomes**
- The shared project repository remains small, fast to download, and easy to navigate.
- No accidental exposure of internal credentials or server configurations.
- Consistent build environments across all team members and deployment pipelines.
- Faster onboarding for new developers and smoother release cycles.
raw_code_snapshot:
# Logs
logs
*.log
npm-debug.log*
yarn-debug.log*
yarn-error.log*
lerna-debug.log*

# Diagnostic reports (https://nodejs.org/api/report.html)
report.[0-9]*.[0-9]*.[0-9]*.[0-9]*.json

# Runtime data
pids
*.pid
*.seed
*.pid.lock

# Directory for instrumented libs generated by jscoverage/JSCover
lib-cov

# Coverage directory used by tools like istanbul
coverage
*.lcov

# nyc test coverage
.nyc_output

# Grunt intermediate storage (https://gruntjs.com/creating-plugins#storing-task-files)
.grunt

# Bower dependency directory (https://bower.io/)
bower_components

# node-waf configuration
.lock-wscript

# Compiled binary addons (https://nodejs.org/api/addons.html)
build/Release

# Dependency directories
node_modules/
jspm_packages/

# Snowpack dependency directory (https://snowpack.dev/)
web_modules/

# TypeScript cache
*.tsbuildinfo

# Optional npm cache directory
.npm

# Optional eslint cache
.eslintcache

# Optional stylelint cache
.stylelintcache

# Optional REPL history
.node_repl_history

# Output of 'npm pack'
*.tgz

# Yarn Integrity file
.yarn-integrity

# dotenv environment variable files
.env
.env.*
!.env.example
.diffdocrc
!.diffdocrc.example

# parcel-bundler cache (https://parceljs.org/)
.cache
.parcel-cache

# Next.js build output
.next
out

# Nuxt.js build / generate output
.nuxt
dist
.output
.diffdoc/vectra

# Gatsby files
.cache/
# Comment in the public line in if your project uses Gatsby and not Next.js
# https://nextjs.org/blog/next-9-1#public-directory-support
# public

# vuepress build output
.vuepress/dist

# vuepress v2.x temp directory
.temp

# Sveltekit cache directory
.svelte-kit/

# vitepress build output
**/.vitepress/dist

# vitepress cache directory
**/.vitepress/cache

# Docusaurus cache and generated files
.docusaurus

# Serverless directories
.serverless/

# FuseBox cache
.fusebox/

# DynamoDB Local files
.dynamodb/

# Firebase cache directory
.firebase/

# TernJS port file
.tern-port

# Stores VSCode versions used for testing VSCode extensions
.vscode-test

# pnpm
.pnpm-store

# yarn v3
.pnp.*
.yarn/*
!.yarn/patches
!.yarn/plugins
!.yarn/releases
!.yarn/sdks
!.yarn/versions

# Vite files
vite.config.js.timestamp-*
vite.config.ts.timestamp-*
.vite/
